title: Present Perfect & Life Experiences — Present Perfect: Form & Use
objectives: Hiểu và áp dụng: present perfect: form & use
Học 24 từ vựng theo chủ đề tuần 5
Hoàn thành 40 câu hỏi trắc nghiệm tăng dần độ khó
Bám sát mục tiêu tuần: Present Perfect & Life Experiences
lesson: **Present Perfect & Life Experiences.** We use the present perfect (have/has + past participle) to talk about **experiences** and actions that connect the past to now. Use it for life **experience** without a specific time: 'I *have visited* Paris'; 'She *has never* tried sushi.' Use *ever* in questions and *never* in negatives: 'Have you *ever* been abroad?' Use *just*, *already* and *yet* to talk about recent actions: 'They *have just* arrived'; 'I haven't finished *yet*.' Use *for* with a period of time and *since* with a point in time: 'We *have lived* here *for* five years / *since* 2015.' Remember 'have been to' (visited and returned) is different from 'have gone to' (still away). Talk about your own **achievements** and **adventures**: what have you done that was **memorable**?
